Title: Machine learning discovery of missing links that mediate alternative branches to plant alkaloids

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Benzylisoquinoline alkaloid intermediate and precursor to medicinal alkaloids/opioids
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports NMCH activity and improves reticuline production when paired with PsNMCH-I1; best same-species redox partner in this study
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Selected by SVM from P. somniferum CPR homologs. 2. Exhibited CPR activity. 3. Paired best with PsNMCH-I1 to yield the highest reticuline among tested NMCH/CPR combinations.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SVM candidate selection; heterologous coexpression with PsNMCH-I1; in vivo norcoclaurine-to-reticuline conversion assay; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No improvement when paired with EcNMCH
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful redox partner for reconstructing plant-gene reticuline pathways in microbes; should be paired with cognate PsNMCH-I1
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TB medium; IPTG induction; fed norcoclaurine; 20-25 C culture
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Annotated as NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase-like; selected over previously characterized but less obvious CPR annotation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Benzylisoquinoline alkaloid intermediate and precursor to medicinal alkaloids/opioids
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports EcNMCH activity; best partner for EcNMCH and also supports engineered EcNMCH-Y202H
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Used as positive/control CPR partner. 2. Best supported reticuline production with EcNMCH. 3. Also supported improved engineered EcNMCH-Y202H activity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous coexpression with EcNMCH/PsNMCH variants; in vivo reticuline assay by LC-MS
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Less optimal than PsCPR-L for PsNMCH-I1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous CPR benchmark for microbial BIA engineering
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TB medium; IPTG induction; fed norcoclaurine
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/control core component in NMCH module comparisons

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Tyrosine/DOPA decarboxylase 1 with aromatic acetaldehyde synthase activity
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent aromatic amino acid decarboxylase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts tyrosine to 4-hydroxyphenylacetaldehyde and L-DOPA to 3,4-dihydroxyphenylacetaldehyde, with bifunctional decarboxylase/AAS behavior
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate aldehyde precursor for norcoclaurine and norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Norcoclaurine and reticuline are key benzylisoquinoline alkaloid intermediates for medicinal alkaloid production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes in vivo norcoclaurine production from tyrosine plus dopamine and contributes synergistically with PsPDC1 to improve reticuline via dual pathways
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SVM prediction identified PsTyDC1 as atypical AAS candidate among PsTyDC homologs. 2. Wild-type PsTyDC1 promoted norcoclaurine production in vivo in E. coli. 3. L205H shifted activity toward tyramine formation, supporting residue-dependent AAS/AAAD specificity. 4. Together with PsPDC1 it enhanced reticuline production through hybrid pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SVM prediction; heterologous expression; active-site mutagenesis; LC-MS metabolite profiling; pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TfNCS; PsONCS3; PsPDC1; PpDDC; Cj6OMT; CjCNMT; Cj4OMT; PsNMCH-I1
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides plant missing-link chemistry for microbial reconstruction of natural-like BIA pathways; can be combined with PPDC templates for higher flux
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: LB/TB media; tyrosine and dopamine or L-DOPA feeding; ascorbate supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Over 98% identical to PsTyDC6 yet lower apparent activity; natural in-plant role remains unresolved

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Tyrosine/DOPA decarboxylase 6 with aromatic acetaldehyde synthase activity
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent aromatic amino acid decarboxylase family
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: AAS branch; dual 4HPAA and DHPAA formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces 4HPAA from tyrosine and DHPAA from L-DOPA; also forms tyramine and dopamine as decarboxylation products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate aldehyde precursor for norcoclaurine and norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Purified recombinant protein and heterologous expression in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: In vitro assay mixture / engineered E. coli culture medium
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central BIA intermediates for downstream medicinal alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Exhibits in vitro AAS activity on tyrosine and L-DOPA and supports formation of norcoclaurine and norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Highest-scoring AAS candidate by SVM despite AAAD-like active-site residues. 2. Purified PsTyDC6 produced 4HPAA, DHPAA and H2O2 in vitro. 3. Also showed bifunctional AAAD activity producing tyramine and dopamine. 4. Demonstrates that plant AAS activity can be hidden in AAAD-like sequences.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SVM prediction; recombinant purification; GC-MS detection of 4HPAA; LC-MS detection of DHPAA/norcoclaurine/norlaudanosoline; H2O2 fluorescent assay
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Bifunctionality may divert flux to amine byproducts
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising enzyme template for designing dual aldehyde-producing steps in alkaloid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro PLP-dependent assays with tyrosine or L-DOPA; 30 C
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shares >98% identity with PsTyDC1 but showed clearer in vitro AAS detection

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Pyruvate decarboxylase 1 with phenylpyruvate decarboxylase activity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate to 4HPAA and likely 3,4-dihydroxyphenylpyruvate to DHPAA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate aldehyde precursor for norcoclaurine and norlaudanosoline
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key missing-link enzyme and alternative branch control point
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: BIA intermediates for medicinal alkaloid production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes alternative 4HPAA bypass and markedly improves norcoclaurine and reticuline production; synergizes with PsTyDC1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified by SVM as a plant PPDC candidate despite PDC-like active-site features. 2. Outperformed PsPDC2 and Ps2HCLL in tyrosol/norcoclaurine production assays. 3. Enabled >10 µM-range norcoclaurine through the 4HPP route. 4. Worked synergistically with PsTyDC1 for higher reticuline and isotope-supported flux through dual pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SVM prediction; heterologous expression; in vivo feeding assays with 4HPP, tyrosine, and L-DOPA; GC-MS/CE-MS/LC-MS metabolite profiling; isotope tracing
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PsTyDC1; PsONCS3; PpDDC; Cj6OMT; CjCNMT; Cj4OMT
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Expression unstable and toxic in E. coli in preliminary experiments; lower activity than ARO10 template
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful plant PPDC missing link for natural-like alkaloid route reconstruction; can be paired with AAS or replaced by high-activity templates depending desired specificity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: M9/LB/TB media; arabinose induction in pBAD system; tyrosine/L-DOPA/4HPP feeding; ascorbate supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains Y332 typical of PDCs, illustrating non-obvious plant PPDC determinants
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabinose-inducible pBAD; T7 in some constructs

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Bottleneck enzyme in tested reticuline module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key BIA intermediate and precursor to medicinal alkaloids/opioids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Afforded higher reticuline than EcNMCH when paired with PsCPR-L or AtATR2; best with cognate PsCPR-L
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Selected automatically by CYP80B-based SVM model. 2. In vivo pathway assays showed higher reticuline production than EcNMCH. 3. Pairing with PsCPR-L gave the highest reticuline. 4. H203 is a functionally important residue; H203Y reduced activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SVM prediction; heterologous coexpression with CPRs and methyltransferases; mutagenesis H203Y; LC-MS reticuline assay
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PsCPR-L; AtATR2; Ps6OMT; PsCNMT; Ps4OMT / Cj methyltransferases in pathway modules
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No detectable early activity without CPR in E. coli; CYP450 expression constraints in bacteria
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Superior plant NMCH option for microbial reticuline synthesis; cognate CPR pairing is recommended
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TB medium; IPTG induction; fed norcoclaurine; 20-25 C culture
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Early L-DOPA observations without CPR were attributed likely to native E. coli HpaBC rather than PsNMCH-I1

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Bottleneck benchmark enzyme in tested module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key BIA intermediate and precursor to medicinal alkaloids/opioids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Conventional NMCH benchmark; less effective than PsNMCH-I1 in this study; best supported by AtATR2
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Served as established NMCH comparator used in previous BIA studies. 2. Produced reticuline in E. coli when paired with CPR. 3. AtATR2 was a better redox partner than PsCPR-L. 4. Y202H engineering improved function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous coexpression with CPR partners; LC-MS reticuline assay; mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower reticuline than PsNMCH-I1 under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful benchmark and engineering template; Y202H variant appears superior
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TB medium; IPTG induction; fed norcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Long-used NMCH in heterologous BIA production systems

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Engineered L-DOPA decarboxylase template with DHPAAS-like activity
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent aromatic amino acid decarboxylase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered to convert L-DOPA toward DHPAA while retaining bifunctional decarboxylase/oxidative deamination behavior
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate aldehyde precursor for norlaudanosoline/reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: BIA intermediates for medicinal alkaloid production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improved norlaudanosoline/reticuline production and, in combinations with ARO10 and PsTyDC1, enabled highest dual-pathway titers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Designed by transplanting DHPAAS-like residues into PpDDC. 2. Increased SVM AAS probability and decreased AAAD probability. 3. Improved norlaudanosoline production in vivo relative to PsTyDC1 template comparison. 4. Supported isotope evidence for bifunctional decarboxylase/oxidative deamination activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; SVM score comparison; in vivo pathway assays; CE-MS/GC-MS isotope and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PsPDC1; ARO10; CjNCS/PsONCS3; Cj6OMT; CjCNMT; Cj4OMT
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Favors DHPAA branch rather than balanced dual production alone
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong enzyme-engineering template for microbial alkaloid production; demonstrates template engineering guided by discovered plant residues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TB or M9 media; L-DOPA feeding; ascorbate supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a native plant gene, but a core engineered enzyme in Results central to target alkaloid production optimization

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Transaminated amino acid decarboxylase / phenylpyruvate decarboxylase template
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates aromatic keto acids such as 4HPP and DHPP to corresponding aryl acetaldehydes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate aldehyde precursor for norcoclaurine/N-methylcoclaurine and reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: BIA intermediates and products for medicinal alkaloid production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improved in vivo turnover of 4HPP and DHPP compared with PsPDC1 and enabled highest norcoclaurine/N-methylcoclaurine titers in 4HPAA route
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Served as high-activity PPDC template replacing PsPDC1. 2. Improved production through both 4HPAA and DHPAA routes. 3. Enabled 356 µM norcoclaurine and 240 µM N-methylcoclaurine in strain A1-01-DE3. 4. Caused specificity tradeoff with byproduct formation from tryptophan-derived indolepyruvate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; pathway reconstruction; GC-MS/CE-MS metabolite profiling; comparison with PsPDC1
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PsTyDC1; PpDDC-Y79F-F80Y-H181N; NCS; Cj6OMT; CjCNMT; Cj4OMT
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower substrate specificity; produced aromatic keto acid-derived byproducts including indole alkaloid byproduct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful high-flux template for microbial alkaloid production when specificity can be managed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TB/M9 media; tyrosine plus dopamine or L-DOPA feeding; gossypol used in one production condition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Template enzyme rather than missing-link plant enzyme, but central to Results optimization
